title: multidevice: directory deduplicates per-user not per-device
description:
Update user_joined, user_left, and directory_list in relay/src/ws.rs so a user appears exactly once regardless of how many devices they have connected.

Why: with multiple connections per pub_id, naive iteration over the clients map would emit user_joined twice for the same pub_id when device 2 connects, and user_left when device 1 disconnects even though device 2 is still online. This confuses all clients about who is present.

What to do:
- user_joined: only broadcast when pub_id transitions from 0 connected devices to 1 (first device to connect). Suppress for subsequent devices of the same pub_id.
- user_left: only broadcast when pub_id transitions from 1 connected device to 0 (last device disconnects). Suppress when a device disconnects but others remain.
- directory_list: deduplicate — collect unique pub_ids from the clients map, not raw connection entries. One entry per pub_id.
- connection_seq for directory ordering: use minimum seq across all devices for a pub_id so ordering is stable regardless of which device connected first this session.

Acceptance: directory_list contains each pub_id exactly once; user_joined fires once per user arrival regardless of device count; user_left fires only when the last device disconnects.
close_reason: connect() returns (seq, is_first_connection); UserJoined suppressed for non-first devices; UserLeft suppressed unless last device; DirectoryList already deduplicated by pub_id key
